here 's a technical explanation : # a brief note on ordinal utility we say that if preferences are complete , transitive , continuous , and locally non-satiated then they can be represented by a utility function . those are the necessary and sufficient conditions . let *x* be the consumption set containing all possible vectors ( consumption bundles ) available to a consumer . let x^0 , x^1 , x^2 ∈ *x* . **1 . ) completeness** ∀ x^0 , x^1 ∈ *x* , either x^0 ⪯ x^1 or x^1 ⪯ x^0 this means for every bundle in the set of all possible bundles , either x^1 is at least as good as x^0 or x^0 is at least as good as x^1 . this basically means that i can form an opinion between any two choices . **2 . ) transitivity** ∀ x^0 , x^1 , x^3 ∈ *x* , if x^0 ⪯ x^1 and x^1 ⪯ x^2 , then x^0 ⪯ x^2 this means that rankings are preferred across bundles . if i prefer one steak and two potatoes better than one steak and one steak and one potato . and if i prefer one steak and one potato better than one steak and one chocolate bar , then i prefer one steak and two potatoes better than one steak and one chocolate bar . **3 . ) continuity** there are many definitions of continuity , so if you want the technical one , i 'm happy to provide it . otherwise , here is the non-technical one : the ⪯ ( x ) set and the ⪰ ( x ) are closed . this is a mainly technical assumption that just happens to hold in almost all cases ( except in the bizarre case of lexicographic preferences , etc ) . this is basically saying that as you move from the `` better than '' to the `` worse than '' set in *x* , you will cross the `` indifferent to '' set , which is the intersection of the `` no worse than '' and `` no better than '' set , and as such , is itself a closed set . **4 . ) local non-satiation** for every x ∈ *x* and for every ε > 0 , there exists some x^0 in an ε-neighborhood of x , such that x^0 ⪰x . this means that no matter where i am in x , there 's always some more-preferred consumption bundle . this rules out `` fat '' zones of indifference or oddly-defined `` bliss points . '' **the function** i really do n't want to go into the proof because it 's long , but the gist of it is this : there exists a utility function u ( x ) such that for all x^1 , x^2 ∈ *x* , u ( x^1 ) ≥ u ( x^2 ) if and only if x^1 ⪰ x^2 . that 's it . note that ***the real-valued `` utils '' do n't matter -- the only thing that matters is the ranking*** . that 's why it 's important to not forget that the domain of a utility function is n't the real line , but rather a binary preference relation , so ordinality is preserved .
